articles.title: Give Your AI Eyes: Introducing Chrome DevTools MCP
articles.description: The Chrome team has released an MCP server for Chrome DevTools, enabling agents like Claude Code or OpenAI Codex to use the DevTools to debug and analyze the performance and behavior of your webapps (or even just to automate the use of Chrome generally). Addy does a great job of explaining the potential here.
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: GitHub's Plan for a More Secure npm Supply Chain
articles.description: In direct response to the recent npm ecosystem supply chain attacks of recent weeks, GitHub’s senior director of security research has outlined steps GitHub is taking, including blocking the upload of packages featuring the patterns of the recent malware, hardening package publication, and promoting the use of trusted publishing.
articles.author: Xavier René-Corail (GitHub)

articles.title: From Steam to Floppy: Porting Modern TypeScript to Run on DOS
articles.description: The creator of a DOS-inspired programming game (available on Steam) wanted to try and get the game running on real DOS. Thanks to jSH, a JavaScript engine for DOS, it was kinda do-able.
articles.author: Jimbly / Dashing Strike

articles.title: NPM Security Best Practices
articles.description: An extensive list of best practices, techniques, and ideas to consider for making your use of the npm packaging ecosystem and its tooling more secure.
articles.author: Boda

articles.title: JSON is Not JSON Across Languages
articles.description: If you use JSON to communicate between systems built in different languages, beware. Different libraries with varying opinions can cause “some of the most soul-crushing debugging experiences in software development.”
articles.author: Dochia CLI

articles.title: 🤖 GitHub Copilot CLI Now in Public Preview
articles.description: Not content to let Claude Code and OpenAI Codex dominate the CLI-based dev agent scene, GitHub has released a CLI-based version of Copilot, built using Node.
articles.author: GitHub

articles.title: TanStack Start v1 Release Candidate
articles.description: TanStack’s attempt at a full-stack TanStack Router-powered framework has reached a v1.0 release candidate that’s expected to be largely the same as its eventual 1.0 release. “It’s the next chapter in building type-safe, high-performance React apps without the heavy abstractions.”
articles.author: Tanner Linsley

articles.title: Cap'n Web: A New RPC System for Browsers and Web Servers
articles.description: A ‘spiritual sibling’ to Cap’n Proto, an RPC protocol created by one of the same authors. However, Cap’n Web’s underlying serialization is human-readable, focused on integrating well with JS runtimes, and works over HTTP, WebSocket, and postMessage() out-of-the-box.
articles.author: Varda and Faulkner (Cloudflare)

articles.title: eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect
articles.description: What a project name! It’s an ESLint plugin to help React developers catch unnecessary useEffects which are quite commonly overused.
articles.author: Nick van Dyke

articles.description: Netscape Navigator 2.0 Beta was released 30 years ago – it was notable as the first browser to include JavaScript support.
The agenda of this week's TC39 meeting where several proposals were advanced, including Import Bytes, Iterator Chunking, and Array.prototype.pushAll.
The Chrome team expects Chrome/Chromium's support for the Temporal API to land in Chromium 144 (the current stable version is 140).
InfoQ shares an update on the features of Preact 11 beta, the recently released update to the popular lightweight React alternative.
The IEEE has released its list of the top programming languages of 2025 with JavaScript and TypeScript coming in at #6 and #7 respectively.
pnpm 10.17 – The new minimumReleaseAgeExclude now supports patterns to exclude groups of packages from minimum release age checks.
Astro 5.14 - Boasts "a bumper harvest of features and DX improvements to warm the cockles of your heart."
Node.js v24.9.0 (Current) and v22.20.0 (LTS)
Nuxt UI 4.0, PostgreSQL 18.0, ESLint v9.36.0

articles.title: What You Need to Know About Modern CSS (2025 Edition)
articles.description: An extensive walk through a list of things which are now valuable to know, because they are “pretty fresh or have enjoyed a boost in browser support”. Covers the popover attribute, the if() function, the new field-sizing property, and more. An excellent overview of what new stuff we should be paying attention to, along with pointers to what’s on the horizon.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: 🔎 The Web’s Most Tolerated Feature
articles.description: Mike talks about the zoom property — once a non-standard IE-only feature, it has since gone on a 25 year journey of sorts to become a modern standard, and a part of Interop 2025. This post recounts that journey, highlighting how it now offers us unique layout capabilities.
articles.author: Mike Pennisi

articles.title: Wasm 3.0 Completed
articles.description: The WebAssembly 3.0 standard is now ‘live’ (and already ships with most browsers). This notable release introduces various features that should improve JS interoperability, including 64-bit address spaces, garbage collection, exception handling, typed references, and more.
articles.author: Andreas Rossberg

articles.title: CSS offset and animation-composition for Rotating Menus
articles.description: Shows us an efficient, relatively straightforward technique for creating circular/radial animated menus using CSS.
articles.author: Preethi Sam

articles.title: Your Images Are (Probably) Oversized
articles.description: Are you using the sizes attribute on your img tags? If not, you may well be serving images that are much larger than necessary.
articles.author: Reason Under Pressure

articles.title: We Keep Reinventing CSS, But Styling Was Never the Problem
articles.description: States that repeated ‘reinvention’ of CSS goes beyond the scope of its original intention, asserting that we’ve now “spent years bolting on strategies to make it work”.
articles.author: Den Odell

articles.title: ▶  iOS 26 Safari, Material Support on the Web, and Fixing the Button Problem
articles.description: The latest version of Safari arrived last week, and as such Chris and Dave discuss it, along with the aesthetics of Liquid Glass, material design, and more.
articles.author: ShopTalk podcast

articles.title: Is it Time to Un-Sass?
articles.description: Jeff notes how CSS is now incorporating new features that first came to us through other third-party tools such as Sass, and how such a change marks a moment to stop and re-evaluate our technology decisions.
articles.author: Jeff Bridgforth

articles.title: Integrating CSS Cascade Layers to an Existing Project
articles.description: Looks at how to approach integrating CSS Cascade Layers into an existing legacy codebase. Essentially, it’s about refactoring existing CSS to use cascade layers without breaking anything.
articles.author: Victor Ayomipo

articles.title: Chrome DevTools (MCP) for Your AI Agent
articles.description: Google’s Chrome team has released a public preview of a new Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for bringing the power of Chrome’s DevTools to AI coding agents. This means tools like Claude Code, Gemini Code or Codex can debug web pages directly using Chrome and benefit from DevTools’ performance insights.
articles.author: Bynens & Hablich (Chrome Developers)

articles.title: repo2txt: Converts GitHub Repository Contents Into a Single Formatted Text File
articles.description: Use it here. Useful for adding your site contents to an LLM prompt. Also has the option to upload a local directory and you can filter/select which files/extensions to include/exclude.
articles.author: Abin Thomas

articles.title: ASCIIFlow: A Web-Based Tool for Drawing ASCII Diagrams
articles.description: The UI resembles your standard graphics editor with features like undo/redo, pan, etc., along with tools to draw arrows, lines, boxes, or even freeform, before exporting in plain text.
articles.author: Lewis Hemens

articles.title: Catalyst: A Next.js Starter Kit for Modern Web Apps
articles.description: The stack includes Bun, Prisma, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Husky, Biome, Auth.js, Stripe, Tabler Icons, and lots more.
articles.author: Richard Kovacs

articles.title: CSS Boilerplate: A Default CSS Structure for Projects of Any Size
articles.description: A simple starter for a CSS file with its main feature being the user of the @layer at-rule for declaring cascade layers.
articles.author: Manuel Matuzović

articles.title: Kigen Color Generator: Create Modern Color Palettes for Your Design Systems
articles.description: Use sliders to choose a base color, from which the tool will generate a palette that you can display using 9 algorithms, among other customizations. Download as SVG or copy in various formats.
articles.author: kigen

articles.title: Logoipsum: 100 Free SVG Placeholder Logos
articles.description: A neat way to add a realistic looking logo to a design temporarily. Allows you to select from 10 different categories and you can interactively preview a chosen logo right on the site’s navbar.
articles.author: Logoipsum
